title: Club long weekend trip to Sheringham, Norfolk, Friday 5th to Monday 8th March 2027 (3 nights)
content: The Club has booked self-catering accommodation in Sheringham on the north Norfolk Coast for a long weekend at the beginning of March 2027. There is excellent coastal walking as well as inland paths to Sheringham Park and Felbrigg Hall. We will offer walks on all four days - but need volunteers to lead B walks on both Saturday and Sunday. The regular coast bus makes it easy to offer linear walks. For those choosing to share a room the cost is £150 per person. Sole occupancy of a room costs £175. If you would like to book, please transfer a deposit of £50 to Jill - if you don't have her email, contact secretary@cambridgeramblingclub.com.

title: Essex coast weekend, Friday 5th to Sunday 7th February 2027
content: The first 'away' trip for 2027 is now open for bookings - although you may want to wait awhile until cheaper 'Advance' rooms are released on the Premier Inns website. We will be visiting and staying at Rayleigh, close to both Canvey Island on the Essex coast and nearby Southend-on-Sea. This is probably a 'first' for most of us! On Friday 5th there will be a walk for everyone from Hadleigh Country Park, about 8.5 miles. On Saturday the walks will be around Canvey Island; an A walk of 14.5 miles is guaranteed, but we can provide routes for a shorter B walk if a leader comes forward. On Sunday we will walk in and around Hockley Woods; an A walk of 13 miles already has a leader, but shorter routes for a B walk are available if leader(s) come forward. We are very grateful to Club members for researching these routes. If you want to take part, please book yourselves into the Basildon/Rayleigh Premier Inn or nearby accommodation and let Jill know. Anyone without a car living in or close to Cambridge should be able to get a lift. Please let Jill know when you have booked.

title: Autumn 'budget' trip - Stamford, Friday 6th to Sunday 8th November 2026
content:
The Club has booked the 'Garden House' in Stamford for a two-night weekend break in early November 2026. This former hotel offers 20 en-suite rooms with self-catering facilities, lounges, indoor games rooms, and a large car park. It is on the south side of the centre, close to both the historic town and Burghley House & Park. We plan walks close to nearby Rutland Water on one day - perhaps the 15 mile circular trail for a leg-stretcher - and also walks through the undulating limestone countryside north of Peterborough, such as Barnack. Relatively close to home, we plan full day walks on 3 days - if we have sufficient offers to lead! Please contact Jill.

Sole occupancy of a room costs £125, with those sharing (either doubles or twins) paying £110 each. A deposit of £40 is required to secure a place - payable to Jill, please. The balance is due by 1st September 2026. All these rooms are now taken, but we are holding a waiting list; please let Jill know if you want to be on it.

You can, of course, book accommodation in Stamford independently - but please do let Jill know if you are coming. And do let Jill know if you want a lift or can offer passenger places in a car. Stamford can also be reached by train from Cambridge.

title: CRC Lakes' trip September 2026 - Ambleside base
content:
The Club's annual Lakes week will be based in Ambleside in September 2026, running from Friday 11th/Saturday 12th to Friday 18th/Saturday 19th depending on your accommodation booking dates. That means there will be group walks on 7 days in total, from Saturday 12th to Friday 18th.

We have decided on this new location for several reasons. Ambleside is right in the heart of the Lakes and is a major public transport hub, with buses to the Langdales, Coniston, Windermere, Kendal and Keswick. Not only does this mean that we can leave our cars parked up, but we have opportunities for many linear walks.

Ambleside has a large selection of self-catering cottages and is very well-provided for with restaurants. Many cottages provide a parking space or permit(s) for on-street or car parks. And we hope to find a venue for the Cambridge group to get together to socialise on a few evenings.

Martin will again be in charge - and thanks so much for his very effective organisation this year! Please let him know when you have booked to come; certainly let Martin know if you are willing to lead a walk: A+, A and B. Contact: lakes@cambridgeramblingclub.com
